hero_tagline: Finding the perfect holiday park near Lennox Head means discovering a place where your family can create wonderful memories together while enjoying the beautiful coastal surroundings and relaxed atmosphere.
heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks in Lennox Head
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip to Lennox Head? We compared the family holiday and caravan park options in Lennox Head so you can quickly find the right base for your kids, budget and travel style.
Lennox Head pairs a genuine surf beach with the unusual bonus of a tea-tree lake next door, so kids get two totally different swims within a five-minute walk of each other.
local_knowledge: Reflections Lennox Head is the town's one holiday park option, sitting directly beside Lake Ainsworth, a tannin-stained tea-tree lake that's calm, warm and completely different to swim in than the ocean, kids float differently in the tea-tree water and love the novelty. The surf beach itself is one of the best right-hand point breaks in Australia, which means older kids keen to learn to surf are well served by local surf schools. The park itself runs light on structured entertainment, so this suits families happy to let the lake and beach do the work. Byron Bay is 20 minutes north if you want more town and shopping.

if_we_were_booking.label: The lake and beach combo
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Reflections Lennox Head for the walk to both Lake Ainsworth's calm tea-tree water and the surf beach, the two-in-one swim that makes Lennox unique.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Try the lake before the ocean. Lake Ainsworth's tea-tree water feels different against the skin and gives younger or nervous swimmers a calm, warm option before tackling the surf beach with older kids.

faq.question: Is Lennox Head good for families?
faq.answer: Yes, especially for families wanting both calm lake swimming and a genuine surf beach within walking distance of each other.

faq.question: What is Lake Ainsworth?
faq.answer: A tea-tree stained freshwater lake beside the holiday park, calm and warm, quite different to swim in than the ocean. It's a local institution.

faq.question: Is the surf beach safe for kids?
faq.answer: It's a genuine surf beach and one of Australia's best point breaks, so it suits confident young swimmers or surf lessons rather than toddler paddling.

faq.question: Does the holiday park have much on-site entertainment?
faq.answer: Limited structured activities outside peak season. The appeal here is the lake and beach rather than resort facilities.

faq.question: How far is Lennox Head from Byron Bay?
faq.answer: About 20 minutes north, an easy day trip for more shopping and dining options.

faq.question: Can kids learn to surf at Lennox Head?
faq.answer: Yes, local surf schools operate on the main beach, well suited to older kids given the quality of the break.

faq.question: What are the best free family activities?
faq.answer: Swimming at Lake Ainsworth, the beach itself, and the headland walk with its whale watching lookout in season.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit?
faq.answer: Autumn and spring for milder weather and smaller surf. Summer brings warmer water but bigger crowds and swell.

faq.question: What should families do when it rains?
faq.answer: Byron Bay's cinema and shops, 20 minutes away, are the practical wet-weather option.

faq.question: How far ahead should you book?
faq.answer: The one park in town fills for school holidays, so book ahead for peak periods.
